# TrailerInsurance.co.nz

> New Zealand's specialist trailer insurance comparison service. We connect Kiwis with ICNZ-registered insurance brokers for free, impartial quotes on all trailer types.

## About

TrailerInsurance.co.nz is a NZ-owned lead generation and comparison platform for trailer insurance. We have 15+ years of industry experience and connect New Zealanders with licensed insurance brokers who provide fast, impartial quotes at no cost to the consumer. We are not an insurer — we are a broker referral service.

**Contact**
- Phone: 09 885 9549
- Email: hello@cover4you.co.nz
- Website: https://trailerinsurance.co.nz

**Headquarters**
- Based in New Zealand
- ICNZ-registered broker network partners
- Licensed under FMA regulations

## What We Cover

### Comprehensive Cover
Full protection against damage, theft, fire, storms, and natural disasters. Covers your trailer 24/7 whether hitched or unhitched.

### Third-Party Liability
Legal liability cover for damage your trailer causes to other people's property or vehicles. Coverage up to $20 million for major incidents.

### Agreed Value
Fixed payout amount in the event of total loss. Ideal for newer trailers or those with known value. No arguments at claims time.

### Market Value
Payout based on the current market value of your trailer at time of claim. Reflects depreciation over time.

### Commercial & Carriers Liability
Specialised cover for business trailers, tools, equipment storage, and heavy transport needs. Covers commercial use of trailers.

### Contents & Accessory Cover
Protection for appliances, camping gear, furniture, and personal items stored in your caravan or enclosed trailer.

### Towing & Storage
Covers emergency towing costs, recovery services, and temporary storage after an accident or breakdown.

### Fire & Theft Protection
Dedicated cover for theft of your trailer or contents. Covers fire damage in all circumstances.

### Accidental Damage Cover
Protection against accidental damage not covered by standard policies.

### Personal Possessions Cover
Coverage for items inside your trailer including camping equipment, furniture, and appliances.

## Trailer Types We Cover

### Caravans
Motorhomes, on-road caravans, and traditional caravans. Full cover including contents protection for modern caravans with full living amenities.
- Page: /insurance/caravan-insurance-nz/

### Boat Trailers
Dedicated insurance for boat trailers used to transport fishing boats, speedboats, and recreational vessels. Covers on-road and at-rest situations.
- Page: /insurance/boat-trailer-insurance-nz/

### Horse Floats
Specialist equine transport insurance protecting horse floats and horse trailers. Includes coverage for regular transport of animals.
- Page: /insurance/horse-float-insurance-nz/

### Camper Trailers
Pop-top caravans and compact camper trailers with sleeping and cooking facilities. Budget-friendly caravanning options.
- Page: /insurance/camper-trailer-insurance-nz/

### Box Trailers
Enclosed cargo and storage trailers used for personal and commercial purposes. Protects contents and trailer structure.
- Page: /insurance/box-trailer-insurance-nz/

### Flatbed Trailers
Open flatbed trailers for transporting equipment, materials, and goods. Commercial and personal use coverage.
- Page: /insurance/flatbed-trailer-insurance-nz/

### Car Trailers
Enclosed car carriers and open car trailers for transporting vehicles. Essential for car dealers and enthusiasts.
- Page: /insurance/car-trailer-insurance-nz/

### Enclosed Trailers
Fully enclosed cargo trailers with lockable doors. Protects valuable contents from weather and theft.
- Page: /insurance/enclosed-trailer-insurance-nz/

## Coverage Types Guide

### Comprehensive Trailer Insurance
The most complete protection available. Covers accidental damage, theft, fire, weather damage, and third-party liability.
- Page: /types/comprehensive/

### Third-Party Liability Insurance
Legal liability only — covers costs when your trailer damages someone else's property. The most basic coverage option.
- Page: /types/third-party-liability/

### Agreed Value vs Market Value
Understanding the difference between fixed payout amounts and depreciated market values helps you choose the right coverage type.
- Page: /types/agreed-value/

### Commercial Trailer Insurance
Specialised cover for business trailers, including carriers liability and commercial use exclusions.
- Page: /types/commercial/

### Contents & Accessory Coverage
Protects the items inside your caravan or trailer including appliances, furniture, and camping gear.
- Page: /types/contents-cover/

### Towing & Storage Coverage
Covers emergency towing, recovery, and temporary storage costs after accidents or breakdowns.
- Page: /types/towing-storage/

## Key Questions We Answer

1. **Do I need separate trailer insurance in NZ?**
   Most car insurance policies only cover your trailer while it's attached to your vehicle. Once unhitched, you're uninsured. Standalone trailer insurance covers you 24/7.

2. **What's the cheapest trailer insurance in NZ?**
   We compare quotes from 10+ ICNZ-registered brokers to find the best rates for your situation. Third-party liability is the cheapest option; comprehensive is most complete.

3. **How much does trailer insurance cost in NZ?**
   Costs depend on trailer type, value, age, coverage level, and usage. Box trailers typically cost $300-600/year; caravans $500-1500/year.

4. **Can I get a caravan insurance quote online?**
   Yes. Our quick form takes 2 minutes and connects you with brokers who provide free quotes within 24 hours.

5. **What's the difference between comprehensive and third-party trailer insurance?**
   Comprehensive covers accidental damage, theft, fire, and weather. Third-party only covers liability for damage you cause to others.

6. **Are my belongings covered in my caravan?**
   Not automatically. You need contents cover to protect items inside your caravan or trailer.

7. **What's agreed value vs market value insurance?**
   Agreed value pays a fixed amount in case of total loss. Market value pays the vehicle's current depreciated value.

8. **Is my horse float covered by car insurance?**
   Typically not adequately. Car insurance may not cover horse float-specific risks or contents.

9. **How do I claim on my trailer insurance?**
   Contact your broker immediately after an incident. Document damage with photos and gather witness statements.

10. **What affects my trailer insurance premium?**
    Trailer type, age, value, safety features (tracking, alarms), claims history, storage location, and annual usage.

11. **Can I insure an older or vintage trailer?**
    Yes. Agreed value coverage works well for vintage trailers with established collector value.

12. **Do I need insurance for a parked caravan?**
    Yes. Your trailer is at highest theft risk when parked. Comprehensive cover protects against theft and weather damage.

13. **What happens if my trailer is towed away?**
    Towing & storage cover reimburses emergency towing and storage costs after accidents.

14. **Are trailers safer in New Zealand?**
    NZ has lower theft rates than Australia, but boat trailers and caravans remain popular theft targets — insurance is essential.

15. **How quickly can I get trailer insurance?**
    Most brokers issue cover within 24 hours of quote acceptance. Some offer same-day cover.

## Coverage Types Explained

### Accidental Damage
Protection for unintended damage from accidents, collisions, or mishaps. Essential for active users.

### Theft & Malicious Damage
Coverage when your trailer is stolen or deliberately damaged. Particularly important for valuable trailers.

### Fire & Natural Disasters
Full cover for fire, lightning, explosion, storm, flood, earthquake, and weather damage.

### Third-Party Property Damage
Legal liability when your trailer damages someone else's property — up to $20M coverage limits.

### Temporary Accommodation
Some caravan policies cover emergency accommodation if your caravan is damaged and unlivable.

### Breakdown & Roadside Assistance
24/7 roadside assistance including towing, lockouts, and fuel delivery in NZ.

### Replacement Parts
New parts used in repairs rather than used or reconditioned parts.

## NZ Regulatory Context

### Insurance Council of New Zealand (ICNZ)
Industry body representing NZ insurers and brokers. All our partnering brokers are ICNZ-registered.
- Website: https://www.icnz.org.nz/

### Financial Markets Authority (FMA)
Regulates financial services in NZ including insurance brokers and claims management.
- Website: https://www.fma.govt.nz/

### Insurance & Financial Services Ombudsman (IFSO)
Independent dispute resolution service for insurance complaints in NZ.
- Website: https://www.ifso.nz/

### Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) Insurance Regulation
RBNZ oversees the prudential regulation of insurers operating in New Zealand.
- Website: https://www.rbnz.govt.nz/regulation-and-supervision/insurers

### Citizens Advice Bureau
Consumer advice and advocacy on insurance rights and complaints.
- Website: https://www.cab.org.nz/

## Trailer Types Reference

**Popular NZ Trailers:**
- On-road caravans (7-20 tonne)
- Pop-top camper trailers
- Horse floats (2-horse and larger)
- Boat trailers (single and tandem axle)
- Box trailers (single and twin axle)
- Flatbed trailers (commercial use)
- Car carriers (open and enclosed)
- Enclosed cargo trailers
- Livestock trailers
- Tandem horse floats
